Unique modern skyscraper built in 1972 and located in the Financial District. While it is no longer the tallest building in San Francisco, it remains the most distinctive skyscraper and is an iconic sight in the city. Great views of it when walking around Chinatown.

The Transamerica Pyramid is one of the most distinctive buildings in the San Francisco skyline. Its sharp, pyramid-shaped design makes it instantly recognizable and has made it an architectural icon of the city for decades.

Seeing it up close in the Financial District is impressive, but it’s also one of those buildings you notice from all over San Francisco. Whether you’re looking at the skyline from Alamo Square, the waterfront, or one of the city’s many hills, the Transamerica Pyramid always stands out.

A few of us from the staff at The Green Cross always enjoy walking through the Financial District and seeing this landmark up close. It’s a great reminder of how unique San Francisco’s skyline really is and definitely worth seeing while exploring the city. 🌆
